components.name: Copper (Cu2+, added as CuCl2/Cu-EDTA)
components.source: main_text
components.evidence: The M. alcaliphilum 20ZR does not grow without copper, and the optimal growth is observed between 5-10 uM of Cu (II)

components.name: Sodium tungstate (Na2WO4)
components.role: tungsten trace element (reduces formate excretion; increases biomass yield)
components.source: main_text
components.evidence: sodium tungsten (Na2WO4, 0.07 mg/L) ... all experiments carried-out in this work were done with the W-supplemented growth medium

components.name: Na2-EDTA (chelator, in trace solution)
components.role: metal chelator (not typically a model exchange)
components.source: stock:P-medium trace solution [ref 9 Demidenko], composition via same 20Z lineage PMC6570963

media.base_medium: Modified 'P medium' (haloalkaliphilic methanotroph mineral medium; nitrate mineral salts, 3% NaCl, carbonate-buffered ~pH 9) as used by the Kalyuzhnaya lab for Methylomicrobium alcaliphilum 20ZR; trace solution cited from Demidenko et al. 2016 (ref 9), which derives from Ojala et al. 2011 (Methods Enzymol.).
media.base_medium_reference: Demidenko A. et al. Fatty acid biosynthesis pathways in Methylomicrobium buryatense 5G(B1). Front. Microbiol. 2016;7:2167 (PMC5222806) [cited as ref 9 for the trace solution]; trace-element composition confirmed via same-lineage 20Z NMS medium in PMC6570963.

sources_chased.what: Target paper main text (PMC5802761) — base salts, phosphate solution, carbonate buffer, copper requirement, tungsten, gas phase

sources_chased.how: WebFetch PMC5222806 — main text points trace solution to Table S1 (DataSheet2.docx); table body not machine-readable in fetch

sources_chased.what: Full trace-element solution composition for M. alcaliphilum 20Z (same Kalyuzhnaya-lab NMS/P-medium lineage)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6570963/ (putrescine engineering paper, same organism/lab)

media.notes: Base salts, phosphate, carbonate buffer, essential copper (5–10 µM) and tungsten (Na2WO4, 0.07 mg/L) come verbatim from the target paper; the 1 ml/L 'trace solution' is cited (ref 9, Demidenko 2016) and its full metal composition was recovered from the same 20Z lineage medium (PMC6570963), adding the previously-missing Ni, Mo, B and W plus EDTA. The nitrogen source is nitrate (KNO3); the 'Ammonium (EX_nh4_e)' entry in current_components is NOT an actual ingredient of this medium. Organism is an aerobic methanotroph grown under O2 limitation (5% O2), so classified aerobic/microaerobic rather than facultative. Final trace-metal molarities left null because the exact ref-9 stock/dilution was not directly readable; salt identities are robust across the lab's 20Z papers.
